title: Purely Citrusy Korean Vegetables Hwajeon
description: A citrusy and molten Korean dish featuring cider vinegar, perfect for a post-workout meal, made in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot. This vegetarian-friendly dish is a variation of traditional hwajeon.
instructions: Preheat the ttukbaegi earthenware pot over medium heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together cider vinegar, korean chili flakes, garlic, and ginger.
Add the sliced zucchini and grated carrots to the bowl and toss to coat with the vinegar mixture.
Add the sesame oil to the preheated ttukbaegi pot and swirl to coat the bottom.
Add the zucchini and carrot mixture to the pot and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve hot.
Traditionally, hwajeon is cooked on a griddle or in a pan, but using a ttukbaegi earthenware pot gives it a unique flavor and texture.
Be careful when handling the hot ttukbaegi pot as it can cause burns.
